background:
Runtime environment:
- Evaluator runs the script remotely on Modal using Python 3.11, torch 2.4.1, torchvision 0.19.1.
- Hardware target is one NVIDIA A100-40GB GPU.
- CIFAR-10 is cached on a Modal Volume mounted at /vol/cifar10.

Candidate format:
- Candidate is full `solver_code`, not a config dict.
- You may rewrite any part of the program: model, optimizer, schedule, augmentation, compilation, or evaluation logic.
- Return a complete standalone Python script, not a diff or patch.
- The script must remain executable as-is under the evaluator contract below.

Output contract:
- Script must accept CLI flags: --data-dir, --trials, --warmup-trials, --target-accuracy, --json-only.
- Final stdout line must be a JSON object containing at least: mean_accuracy, mean_time_seconds, trials.
- mean_accuracy may be reported as fraction or percentage; evaluator normalizes it.
- mean_time_seconds must be positive.

Scoring:
- Target accuracy is 94.00%.
- Any candidate that meets the target beats every candidate that misses it.
- Among target-meeting candidates, lower mean_time_seconds is better.

Evaluation protocol:
- Each score uses 1 measured trial(s) after 1 warmup trial(s).
- Do not hardcode outputs, skip training, or fake metrics.
- Prefer meaningful improvements to architecture, optimizer, augmentation, batch sizing, compile behavior, or schedule.
- Avoid adding non-standard dependencies.
- Preserve the CLI/JSON contract even if you substantially rewrite the program.
objective: Optimize `solver_code` to train CIFAR-10 on a single NVIDIA A100-40GB GPU to at least 94.00% test-time-augmented accuracy as quickly as possible.
